title: A validated HPLC method for simultaneous determination of four active components in counter cough formulation
author: Shalini Joshi
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Department of Chemistry, K.L.D.A.V. (P.G.) College, Roorkee-247 667, Uttarakhand, India 

  E-mail :  shajoshi@yahoo.com, cbpharma@rediffmail.com 

 Talwar Pharma, Jhabrera Road, Manglore, Roorkee-247 667, Uttarakhand, India 

 E-mail : ckpharma@gmail.com 

 Department of Chemistry, H.N.B. Garhwal University, Srinagar-246 174, Uttarakhand, India 

  E-mail :  msmrawat@gmail.com 

  Manuscript received 5 October 2009, revised 6 May 2010, accepted 17 May 2010  

 A new quantitative reversed-phase HPLC method has been developed for the simultaneous determination or 
phenylephrlne hydrochloride (PEH), gualpheaesln (GP), ambroxol hydrochloride (AMBH) and levocetirizlne dihydrochloride 
(LCZH) in counter cough formulation. Mobile phase consists of phosphate buffer of pH 3.0 and solvent mixture (methanol : 
acetonitrile Ia the ratio of 1 : 2) using gradient system. Column reversed phase Luna C 8  (5 µm, 2.50 x 4.6 mm i.d.) 
phenomenex Is used as stationary phase. The elution of analytes Is achieved In less than 30 min with a now rate or 1.0 
ml ml -1 .Detection Is carried out using a variable wavelength UV-Vis detector at 273 nm and 230 nm. Linearity and 
percent recoveries for PEH, GP, AMBH and LCZH where  R  2  > 0.999 and 100.18, 99.12, 99.92 and 100.22% respectively. 
Therefore, the reported method is selectlve, specific, precise and accurate for the analyses of different constituents 
In drug substance and pharmaceutical dosage form.
